articles.headline: Nation Pays Homage on Shaheed Diwas: Remembering Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev
articles.explainer:
• India observes Shaheed Diwas (Martyr's Day) on March 23rd, commemorating the martyrdom of revolutionary heroes Bhagat Singh, Shivaram Rajguru, and Sukhdev Thapar.
• The trio was executed by the British in 1931 for their role in the Lahore Conspiracy Case and the assassination of John Saunders.
• Their sacrifice ignited nationalist fervor and inspired countless Indians in the struggle for independence.
• Discussions often revolve around their socialist ideals, revolutionary philosophy, and enduring relevance for youth and governance.
• Various events, seminars, and tributes are held across the country to honor their courage and vision.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues
The Freedom Struggle - its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country
prelimsPoints: Shaheed Diwas is observed on March 23rd.
Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev were executed on March 23, 1931.
They were associated with the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA).
The trio was involved in the Lahore Conspiracy Case.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ideological contributions of Bhagat Singh and his comrades to the Indian freedom struggle.
Analyze the impact of the revolutionary movement on the broader nationalist movement.
Examine the contemporary relevance of the ideals of justice, equality, and sacrifice espoused by these martyrs.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev Thapar with a backdrop of the Indian flag.
articles.trivia: Bhagat Singh famously said, "The aim of life is no longer to go to heaven, but to establish a heaven on earth."

articles.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces 'Right to Be Forgotten' in Digital Age
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, has clarified the scope and application of the 'Right to Be Forgotten' in India, particularly concerning public records and digital platforms.
• The judgment emphasizes balancing an individual's right to privacy with the public interest in access to information and freedom of expression.
• It mandates clear guidelines for data fiduciaries on delisting or de-indexing personal information that is no longer relevant or accurate.
• This ruling is seen as a significant step towards strengthening data protection and individual autonomy in the digital realm.
• The court highlighted the need for a comprehensive data protection law to fully address such evolving digital rights.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The 'Right to Be Forgotten' is an evolving aspect of the Right to Privacy (Article 21).
It allows individuals to request removal of personal data from public view after a certain period or if it's no longer relevant.
The Supreme Court's ruling provides specific guidelines for its implementation.
It aims to balance individual privacy with freedom of speech and public information access.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the 'Right to Be Forgotten' for data privacy and freedom of expression in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the 'Right to Be Forgotten' in the absence of a robust data protection framework.
Evaluate how this judgment aligns with global trends in digital rights and data governance.
articles.imageDescription: Gavel on top of legal documents with a blurred digital background representing data.
articles.trivia: The 'Right to Be Forgotten' originated from the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

articles.headline: India and France Deepen Strategic Ties with Focus on Indo-Pacific and Defence Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• India and France concluded their annual Strategic Dialogue, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region, defense, and emerging technologies.
• Discussions focused on maritime security, counter-terrorism efforts, and joint exercises, underscoring a shared vision for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to strengthening 'Make in India' initiatives in the defense sector, including technology transfer and co-production.
• The dialogue also covered collaboration in space, nuclear energy, and climate change, highlighting the multifaceted nature of their strategic partnership.
• This engagement reinforces India's position as a key global player and France's crucial role in India's strategic autonomy.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France share a long-standing strategic partnership.
The Indo-Pacific region is a key area of cooperation for both nations.
Defence collaboration includes technology transfer and joint manufacturing.
Cooperation extends to space, nuclear energy, and climate change.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the context of evolving Indo-Pacific geopolitics.
Evaluate the role of defence cooperation in strengthening India's strategic autonomy and France's positioning in the region.
Discuss how India's bilateral relations with major powers like France contribute to its multilateral engagements and global influence.
articles.imageDescription: Indian and French flags displayed together at a diplomatic event.
articles.trivia: France was the first P5 nation to establish a strategic partnership with India in 1998.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Advanced Semi-Cryogenic Engine for Future Heavy-Lift Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful qualification test of its new generation semi-cryogenic engine, crucial for future heavy-lift launch vehicles.
• This engine, powered by refined kerosene (RP-1) and liquid oxygen, offers higher thrust and efficiency compared to conventional engines, reducing launch costs.
• The development is a significant step towards enhancing India's independent access to space and capability to launch heavier payloads, including future manned missions.
• It will power the future stages of India's next-generation launch vehicles, potentially replacing older configurations.
• This achievement underscores India's growing self-reliance and technological prowess in advanced rocket propulsion systems.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Semi-cryogenic engines use refined kerosene (RP-1) and liquid oxygen as propellants.
They offer higher thrust and are more cost-effective than fully cryogenic engines.
ISRO's successful test aims to enhance India's heavy-lift launch capabilities.
This technology is vital for future missions, including potential human spaceflight.
mainsPoints: Assess the significance of ISRO's advancements in semi-cryogenic engine technology for India's space program and its global standing.
Discuss how indigenous development of advanced propulsion systems contributes to India's strategic autonomy in space.
Examine the potential applications and benefits of heavy-lift launch capabilities for India's economic and security interests.
articles.imageDescription: A schematic diagram of a semi-cryogenic rocket engine being tested.
articles.trivia: ISRO aims to integrate its semi-cryogenic engine into the future variants of the GSLV Mk III (LVM3) launch vehicle.

articles.headline: Government Launches Framework for National Mission on Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS)
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has unveiled a comprehensive framework for the National Mission on Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS).
• The mission aims to promote research, development, and deployment of CCUS technologies to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and power generation.
• It includes provisions for pilot projects, fiscal incentives for industrial adoption, and international collaborations for technology transfer.
• CCUS is identified as a critical tool in India's strategy to achieve its net-zero emissions targets by 2070, complementing renewable energy expansion.
• Experts highlight the need for robust regulatory mechanisms and significant investment to scale up CCUS infrastructure effectively.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: CCUS technologies aim to capture CO2 emissions from industrial sources.
Captured CO2 can be utilized for various purposes or stored underground.
The National Mission on CCUS targets hard-to-abate sectors.
It is a key component of India's strategy to achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS) technologies in achieving India's climate targets, particularly in hard-to-abate sectors.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with the implementation of a national CCUS mission in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of CCUS as a climate mitigation strategy compared to renewable energy and energy efficiency measures.
articles.imageDescription: Industrial plant with carbon capture technology chimneys releasing clean air.
articles.trivia: Globally, CCUS projects currently capture only a small fraction of annual industrial CO2 emissions, but the technology is rapidly developing.

articles.headline: India's Q4 FY25 GDP Growth Projections Show Robust Manufacturing Sector Performance
articles.explainer:
• Leading economic think tanks and rating agencies have released their Q4 FY25 GDP growth projections for India, indicating continued robust expansion.
• The manufacturing sector is highlighted as a key driver, benefiting from sustained government incentives, infrastructure push, and global demand recovery.
• Services sector growth also remains strong, contributing significantly to overall economic momentum.
• While inflation remains a concern, favorable monsoon forecasts and stable global commodity prices are expected to support growth.
• These projections reinforce India's position as one of the fastest-growing major economies globally, attracting significant foreign investment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: GDP is the total monetary or market value of all the finished goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific time period.
The manufacturing sector's performance is a key indicator of economic health.
Government policies like PLI (Production Linked Incentive) schemes boost manufacturing.
Services sector is also a major contributor to India's GDP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's sustained GDP growth in FY25, with a special focus on the manufacturing sector.
Discuss the challenges that India's economy might face despite robust growth projections, such as inflation and global economic uncertainties.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies in boosting manufacturing and attracting foreign direct investment.
articles.imageDescription: Financial graphs and charts overlaid on a map of India, symbolizing economic growth.
articles.trivia: India is projected to become the world's third-largest economy by 2027.

articles.headline: Coastal Security Grid to be Further Strengthened with Advanced Surveillance and Interception Capabilities
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs has announced a new phase of initiatives to bolster India's coastal security grid, drawing lessons from past threats.
• The plan involves deploying advanced surveillance technologies, including drone patrols and integrated radar systems, along the entire coastline.
• Enhanced coordination mechanisms among the Indian Navy, Coast Guard, Marine Police, and other stakeholders are a key focus.
• Training and capacity building for coastal communities and fishermen, who act as 'eyes and ears' for security forces, will be intensified.
• The move aims to prevent infiltration, smuggling, and maritime terrorism, safeguarding India's vast coastline and economic interests.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: India has a coastline of approximately 7,516 km.
Coastal security involves multiple agencies like the Indian Navy, Coast Guard, and Marine Police.
Advanced surveillance technologies are being integrated into the coastal security framework.
Community participation, especially from fishermen, is crucial for effective coastal security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted challenges to India's coastal security and the measures being taken to address them.
Evaluate the role of technology and inter-agency coordination in strengthening India's maritime domain awareness and response capabilities.
Examine how community involvement can be effectively leveraged to enhance coastal security and counter illicit activities.
articles.imageDescription: Indian Coast Guard patrol boat on surveillance duty in coastal waters.
articles.trivia: India's coastline spans nine states and four Union Territories.

articles.headline: Government Expands 'Poshan 2.0' with Special Focus on Adolescent Health and Nutrition
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development has announced the expansion of the 'Poshan 2.0' mission to include a dedicated focus on improving the health and nutritional status of adolescents.
• This phase will integrate programs addressing anemia, menstrual hygiene, and nutritional awareness among adolescent girls and boys.
• The initiative aims to leverage technology for monitoring nutritional outcomes and ensuring last-mile delivery of services through Anganwadi centers and schools.
• Recognizing the critical link between adolescent health and the nation's future human capital, this move seeks to break the intergenerational cycle of malnutrition.
• Public-private partnerships and community engagement are emphasized for effective outreach and sustainable impact.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018.
Poshan 2.0 integrates various nutrition-related schemes.
This phase specifically targets adolescent health and nutrition.
Anemia and menstrual hygiene are key areas of focus for adolescent girls.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the 'Poshan 2.0' mission's expanded focus on adolescent health and nutrition in addressing India's malnutrition challenges.
Discuss the socio-economic implications of adolescent malnutrition and how targeted interventions can contribute to human capital development.
Examine the role of inter-sectoral convergence and community participation in ensuring the success of large-scale nutrition programs like Poshan Abhiyaan.
articles.imageDescription: Group of healthy adolescents participating in an awareness program on nutrition.
articles.trivia: India has one of the highest burdens of adolescent anemia globally.

articles.headline: Horticulture Sector Mourns Demise of Dr. Krishna Lal Chadha, 'Father of Indian Horticulture'
articles.explainer:
• Dr. Krishna Lal Chadha, renowned horticultural scientist and former Deputy Director General (Horticulture) of ICAR, passed away at 88.
• He was widely regarded as the 'Father of Indian Horticulture' for his pioneering contributions to research, development, and policy-making in the field.
• His work significantly boosted the production and productivity of fruits, vegetables, and ornamental crops across India.
• Dr. Chadha played a pivotal role in establishing several horticultural research institutions and promoting commercial horticulture.
• His legacy continues to influence agricultural practices and food security initiatives in the country.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: Dr. Krishna Lal Chadha was a renowned horticultural scientist.
He served as Deputy Director General (Horticulture) of ICAR.
He is known for his significant contributions to Indian horticulture.
His work focused on increasing fruit, vegetable, and ornamental crop production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of scientific research and agricultural scientists like Dr. K.L. Chadha in transforming India's horticulture sector.
Analyze the importance of horticulture in ensuring food security, farmer income, and agricultural diversification in India.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in promoting sustainable and commercial horticulture practices in the country.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Dr. Krishna Lal Chadha with a background of vibrant fruit orchards.
articles.trivia: Dr. Chadha was instrumental in the development of new varieties of mango, grapes, and litchi.

articles.headline: Government Considers New Policy Framework to Regulate Generative AI
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is reportedly drafting a comprehensive policy framework to regulate Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The framework aims to address concerns related to ethical AI development, data privacy, misinformation, copyright infringement, and job displacement.
• It is expected to propose guidelines for AI developers, deployers, and users, fostering responsible innovation while mitigating potential risks.
• Consultations with industry experts, academia, and civil society organizations are underway to ensure a balanced and future-ready policy.
• India seeks to position itself as a global leader in ethical AI, leveraging its digital public infrastructure while ensuring user safety and trust.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Generative AI refers to AI models that can generate new content like text, images, or code.
MeitY is the nodal ministry for IT and electronics policy in India.
The new policy aims to balance innovation with ethical concerns and risks.
Issues like data privacy, misinformation, and intellectual property are key considerations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical and regulatory challenges posed by the rapid advancement of Generative AI and the need for a comprehensive policy framework in India.
Analyze how India can leverage its digital public infrastructure to foster responsible AI innovation while mitigating risks like misinformation and job displacement.
Evaluate the potential impact of AI regulation on India's technology sector and its ambitions to become a global AI hub.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract representation of AI neural networks and data flowing through digital circuits.
articles.trivia: Generative AI models like ChatGPT and Midjourney gained widespread public attention in 2022-2023.

articles.headline: India Pushes for Coordinated G20 Action on Global Debt Crisis
articles.explainer:
• India, as a prominent voice in the Global South, is actively advocating for a coordinated G20 approach to address the escalating global debt crisis, particularly affecting developing nations.
• During recent G20 Finance Ministers' meetings, India emphasized the need for quicker debt restructuring, increased liquidity, and sustainable financing mechanisms.
• The discussions focused on improving the Common Framework for Debt Treatments and expanding its scope to include middle-income countries.
• India also highlighted the importance of multilateral development banks playing a larger role in providing concessional financing and technical assistance.
• This push aligns with India's broader foreign policy goal of championing the interests of developing economies on global platforms.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
India has been a strong advocate for developing countries' concerns in the G20.
The Common Framework for Debt Treatments is an initiative by the G20 and Paris Club.
Global debt crisis affects many low and middle-income countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and objectives in advocating for a coordinated G20 response to the global debt crisis, particularly for developing nations.
Discuss the challenges faced by developing countries due to unsustainable debt burdens and the limitations of existing international debt resolution mechanisms.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multilateral forums like the G20 in addressing complex global economic issues and fostering financial stability.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo with world map background, symbolizing global economic cooperation.
articles.trivia: The G20 accounts for about 85% of global GDP, 75% of global trade, and two-thirds of the world's population.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Progress in Pilot Projects and Policy Incentives
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy released a progress report on the National Green Hydrogen Mission, highlighting significant strides in pilot project implementation.
• Several large-scale green hydrogen production facilities are under construction, leveraging India's renewable energy potential.
• The mission's policy incentives, including SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme, are attracting private investment and fostering technological innovation.
• Focus remains on reducing production costs, developing robust storage and transportation infrastructure, and promoting indigenous manufacturing of electrolyzers.
• Green hydrogen is envisioned as a crucial element for decarbonizing hard-to-abate industrial sectors and achieving energy independence.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
The SIGHT programme provides financial incentives for green hydrogen manufacturing.
It is crucial for decarbonizing industries like refining, fertilizer, and steel.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the progress and potential of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving energy security and climate goals.
Discuss the technological and economic challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production, storage, and distribution infrastructure in India.
Analyze the role of government incentives and private sector investment in driving the green hydrogen ecosystem and promoting indigenous manufacturing.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.

articles.headline: Smart Cities Mission 2.0 to Prioritize Climate-Resilient Urban Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has indicated that the upcoming phase of the Smart Cities Mission (potentially 'Smart Cities Mission 2.0') will place a strong emphasis on climate-resilient urban infrastructure.
• The focus will be on integrating green building standards, sustainable mobility solutions, efficient waste management, and climate-adaptive urban planning.
• The initiative aims to equip Indian cities to better withstand the impacts of climate change, such as extreme weather events and resource scarcity.
• Enhanced digital governance and citizen participation will remain core tenets, ensuring inclusive and sustainable urban development.
• This strategic shift reflects India's commitment to achieving its climate goals and building livable, future-proof urban centers.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015.
The new phase will prioritize climate resilience in urban infrastructure.
Focus areas include green buildings, sustainable mobility, and waste management.
It aims to make cities better prepared for climate change impacts.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of India's Smart Cities Mission and the significance of its renewed focus on climate-resilient urban infrastructure.
Discuss the challenges faced by Indian cities in adapting to climate change and how smart urban planning can mitigate these risks.
Evaluate the role of technology and citizen participation in fostering sustainable and livable cities in the context of climate change.
articles.imageDescription: Modern cityscape with green spaces, solar panels, and smart traffic systems.
articles.trivia: The first phase of the Smart Cities Mission aimed to develop 100 smart cities across India.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Review of Implementation in Higher Education Sector
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an interim review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in the higher education sector.
• The report highlights progress in curriculum reforms, multidisciplinary education, academic bank of credits, and increased autonomy for higher education institutions.
• Challenges identified include faculty readiness, infrastructure gaps, digital divide in remote areas, and the need for greater industry-academia collaboration.
• Recommendations include accelerated faculty development programs, increased digital infrastructure investment, and enhanced funding mechanisms.
• The NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system, making it more holistic, flexible, and aligned with 21st-century skills.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 was approved by the Union Cabinet in 2020.
It replaces the National Policy on Education of 1986.
Key features for higher education include multidisciplinary approach and Academic Bank of Credits.
The review assesses implementation progress and identifies challenges.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges in the implementation of National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in the higher education sector.
Discuss how the NEP 2020 aims to address the skill gap and enhance the employability of graduates in the rapidly changing global economy.
Suggest measures to overcome the identified challenges and ensure the successful transformation of India's higher education system as envisioned by NEP 2020.
articles.imageDescription: Students in a modern university setting, engaged in learning and discussion.
articles.trivia: NEP 2020 aims for 50% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education by 2035.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves New Milestone in Health ID Generation
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced a significant milestone, surpassing 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHAs) or Health IDs.
• This achievement marks rapid progress in digitizing India's healthcare ecosystem, enabling seamless access to health records and services.
• ABHA acts as a unique identifier for individuals, linking their health records across different healthcare providers and allowing secure data sharing.
• The mission aims to create a comprehensive digital health infrastructure, improving efficiency, accessibility, and quality of healthcare services.
• Efforts are underway to onboard more healthcare providers and integrate various health programs under the ABDM framework.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched in September 2021.
It aims to create a digital health ecosystem in India.
ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) is a unique 14-digit health ID.
It links individual health records across various healthcare providers.
mainsPoints: Assess the significance of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in transforming India's healthcare delivery system and improving health outcomes.
Discuss the challenges involved in digitizing health records across a vast and diverse country like India, including data privacy and digital literacy.
Evaluate how the ABDM can contribute to universal health coverage and enhance the efficiency and accessibility of healthcare services for all citizens.
articles.imageDescription: Digital interface displaying a health record with a QR code and medical symbols.
articles.trivia: The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.

articles.headline: Shihan Hussaini, Renowned Actor and Karateka, Passes Away at 60
articles.explainer:
• Shihan Hussaini, a celebrated Indian actor, director, and martial arts exponent, passed away at the age of 60.
• Known for his contributions to Tamil cinema, he was also a highly respected karateka, holding a high-ranking black belt.
• He founded the Shihan Hussaini Karate School, training thousands of students and promoting martial arts discipline across the country.
• Hussaini often integrated his martial arts skills into his film roles, creating a unique niche for himself.
• His demise is mourned by the film fraternity and the martial arts community, remembering his dedication to both arts.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
articles.imageDescription: Portrait of Shihan Hussaini in a karate gi.
articles.trivia: Shihan means 'master instructor' or 'senior instructor' in Japanese martial arts.

articles.headline: Actor and Director Manoj Bharathiraja Passes Away at 48
articles.explainer:
• Manoj Bharathiraja, a well-known actor and director in the Tamil film industry, passed away at the age of 48.
• Son of veteran filmmaker Bharathiraja, he made his debut as an actor and later ventured into direction.
• He was recognized for his versatile performances and his contributions to Tamil cinema in various capacities.
• His passing marks a loss for the regional film industry, with many colleagues and fans expressing condolences.
• He continued his family's legacy in filmmaking, leaving behind a body of work cherished by his audience.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
articles.trivia: His father, Bharathiraja, is a highly acclaimed director known for his realistic portrayal of rural life.

articles.headline: Former Bihar MLA and Islamic Scholar Syed Ali Ashrafi Passes Away
articles.explainer:
• Syed Ali Ashrafi, a former Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Bihar and a respected Islamic scholar, passed away.
• He was known for his political career and significant contributions to religious education and community service.
• Ashrafi played a role in advocating for minority rights and promoting interfaith harmony in his region.
• His demise is mourned by political leaders and community members, recognizing his long-standing public service.
• He leaves behind a legacy of scholarship and dedication to social causes.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
articles.trivia: Bihar has a bicameral legislature, consisting of the Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) and the Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad).

highlights: Nation observes Shaheed Diwas on March 23rd, paying tribute to Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev for their ultimate sacrifice in the freedom struggle.
Supreme Court reinforces 'Right to Be Forgotten' in a landmark ruling, balancing privacy with public interest in the digital age.
India and France strengthen strategic partnership with focus on Indo-Pacific security and advanced defence cooperation.
ISRO successfully tests an advanced semi-cryogenic engine, enhancing India's heavy-lift launch capabilities for future space missions.
Government launches framework for National Mission on Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS) to achieve net-zero emissions targets.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to Shaheed Diwas observed on March 23rd, consider the following statements:
1. It commemorates the execution of Mahatma Gandhi by the British.
2. Bhagat Singh, Shivaram Rajguru, and Sukhdev Thapar were executed on this day in 1931.
3. They were associated with the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. Shaheed Diwas on March 23rd commemorates the execution of Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev. Mahatma Gandhi's martyrdom is observed on January 30th. Statements 2 and 3 are correct. Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev Thapar were executed on March 23, 1931, and were prominent members of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA).

prelimsMocks.question:
The 'Right to Be Forgotten' is an evolving concept in India, often linked to which of the following Fundamental Rights?
(a) Right to Equality (Article 14)
(b) Right to Freedom of Speech and Expression (Article 19)
(c) Right to Life and Personal Liberty (Article 21)
(d) Right to Constitutional Remedies (Article 32)
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Right to Life and Personal Liberty (Article 21)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The 'Right to Be Forgotten' is considered an integral part of the Right to Privacy, which itself has been recognized as a fundamental right under Article 21 (Right to Life and Personal Liberty) by the Supreme Court of India in the Puttaswamy judgment.

prelimsMocks.question:
Which of the following propellants are typically used in a semi-cryogenic rocket engine like the one successfully tested by ISRO?
(a) Liquid Hydrogen and Liquid Oxygen
(b) Solid Propellant and Liquid Oxygen
(c) Refined Kerosene (RP-1) and Liquid Oxygen
(d) Hydrazine and Nitrogen Tetroxide
prelimsMocks.explanation: Semi-cryogenic engines primarily use refined kerosene (RP-1) as fuel and liquid oxygen as the oxidizer. Fully cryogenic engines use liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen. Solid propellants and hypergolic propellants (like Hydrazine and Nitrogen Tetroxide) are used in other types of rocket engines.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Green Hydrogen Mission:
1. Its primary aim is to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
2. The 'SIGHT' programme provides financial incentives for the manufacturing of electrolyzers and green hydrogen production.
3. Green hydrogen is produced by the electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: All three statements are correct. The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to establish India as a leader in green hydrogen. The SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) programme provides key incentives. Green hydrogen is indeed produced through electrolysis powered by renewable energy, ensuring a low carbon footprint.

prelimsMocks.question:
Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA) is a unique identifier associated with which of the following government initiatives?
(a) National Health Mission
(b) Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana
(c) Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission
(d) National Rural Health Mission
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Ayushman Bharat Health Account (ABHA), a unique 14-digit health ID, is a core component of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM). It aims to create a digital health ecosystem where individuals can access and share their health records seamlessly.

mainsMocks.question: Examine the ideological contributions of Bhagat Singh and his comrades to the Indian freedom struggle. How does their revolutionary philosophy continue to inspire contemporary movements for social justice and equality?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues. The Freedom Struggle - its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Bhagat Singh and his ideology (socialism, secularism, anti-imperialism). Discuss their methods (revolutionary acts, propaganda, self-sacrifice). Link their ideals to contemporary issues of social justice, youth activism, and challenging oppressive systems. Conclude on their enduring legacy.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's reinforcement of the 'Right to Be Forgotten' marks a significant step towards strengthening data protection in India. Critically analyze the challenges in balancing an individual's right to privacy with public interest and freedom of expression in the digital age.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure. Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary. Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by defining the 'Right to Be Forgotten' and its origin. Discuss the Supreme Court's ruling and its implications for data privacy. Analyze the inherent conflict with freedom of expression, historical record, and public interest. Highlight practical challenges in implementation and the need for a robust data protection law. Conclude with measures for a balanced approach.

mainsMocks.question: Discuss the strategic imperatives driving India's enhanced defence and Indo-Pacific cooperation with France. How does this partnership contribute to India's strategic autonomy and its broader foreign policy objectives?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: India and its neighborhood- relations. Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests. Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the India-France strategic partnership and its historical context. Detail the key areas of cooperation, especially defence (technology transfer, co-production) and Indo-Pacific strategy (maritime security, counter-terrorism). Explain how this bolsters India's strategic autonomy by diversifying its defence sources and enhancing its regional influence. Connect it to India's multilateral engagements and role as a net security provider. Conclude on the future trajectory of this critical partnership.

quickBytes.text: Shaheed Diwas on March 23rd commemorates Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev's sacrifice.
The 'Right to Be Forgotten' is an extension of the Right to Privacy under Article 21.
India's coastline is approximately 7,516 km long, critical for national security.
Dr. Krishna Lal Chadha is known as the 'Father of Indian Horticulture'.
Semi-cryogenic engines use refined kerosene (RP-1) and liquid oxygen as propellants.
The G20 represents about 85% of global GDP and two-thirds of the world's population.
National Green Hydrogen Mission aims for 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen by 2030.
The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015 for urban development.
NEP 2020 aims for 50% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education by 2035.
ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) is a 14-digit unique health ID.

GS1.headline: Nation Pays Homage on Shaheed Diwas: Remembering Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev
GS1.explainer:
• India observes Shaheed Diwas (Martyr's Day) on March 23rd, commemorating the martyrdom of revolutionary heroes Bhagat Singh, Shivaram Rajguru, and Sukhdev Thapar.
• The trio was executed by the British in 1931 for their role in the Lahore Conspiracy Case and the assassination of John Saunders.
• Their sacrifice ignited nationalist fervor and inspired countless Indians in the struggle for independence.
• Discussions often revolve around their socialist ideals, revolutionary philosophy, and enduring relevance for youth and governance.
• Various events, seminars, and tributes are held across the country to honor their courage and vision.
gsSubTopics: Modern Indian history from about the middle of the eighteenth century until the present- significant events, personalities, issues
The Freedom Struggle - its various stages and important contributors/contributions from different parts of the country
prelimsPoints: Shaheed Diwas is observed on March 23rd.
Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev were executed on March 23, 1931.
They were associated with the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA).
The trio was involved in the Lahore Conspiracy Case.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ideological contributions of Bhagat Singh and his comrades to the Indian freedom struggle.
Analyze the impact of the revolutionary movement on the broader nationalist movement.
Examine the contemporary relevance of the ideals of justice, equality, and sacrifice espoused by these martyrs.
GS1.imageDescription: Illustration of Bhagat Singh, Rajguru, and Sukhdev Thapar with a backdrop of the Indian flag.
GS1.trivia: Bhagat Singh famously said, "The aim of life is no longer to go to heaven, but to establish a heaven on earth."

GS1.headline: Smart Cities Mission 2.0 to Prioritize Climate-Resilient Urban Infrastructure
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs has indicated that the upcoming phase of the Smart Cities Mission (potentially 'Smart Cities Mission 2.0') will place a strong emphasis on climate-resilient urban infrastructure.
• The focus will be on integrating green building standards, sustainable mobility solutions, efficient waste management, and climate-adaptive urban planning.
• The initiative aims to equip Indian cities to better withstand the impacts of climate change, such as extreme weather events and resource scarcity.
• Enhanced digital governance and citizen participation will remain core tenets, ensuring inclusive and sustainable urban development.
• This strategic shift reflects India's commitment to achieving its climate goals and building livable, future-proof urban centers.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
prelimsPoints: The Smart Cities Mission was launched in 2015.
The new phase will prioritize climate resilience in urban infrastructure.
Focus areas include green buildings, sustainable mobility, and waste management.
It aims to make cities better prepared for climate change impacts.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolution of India's Smart Cities Mission and the significance of its renewed focus on climate-resilient urban infrastructure.
Discuss the challenges faced by Indian cities in adapting to climate change and how smart urban planning can mitigate these risks.
Evaluate the role of technology and citizen participation in fostering sustainable and livable cities in the context of climate change.
GS1.imageDescription: Modern cityscape with green spaces, solar panels, and smart traffic systems.
GS1.trivia: The first phase of the Smart Cities Mission aimed to develop 100 smart cities across India.

GS1.headline: Shihan Hussaini, Renowned Actor and Karateka, Passes Away at 60
GS1.explainer:
• Shihan Hussaini, a celebrated Indian actor, director, and martial arts exponent, passed away at the age of 60.
• Known for his contributions to Tamil cinema, he was also a highly respected karateka, holding a high-ranking black belt.
• He founded the Shihan Hussaini Karate School, training thousands of students and promoting martial arts discipline across the country.
• Hussaini often integrated his martial arts skills into his film roles, creating a unique niche for himself.
• His demise is mourned by the film fraternity and the martial arts community, remembering his dedication to both arts.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
GS1.imageDescription: Portrait of Shihan Hussaini in a karate gi.
GS1.trivia: Shihan means 'master instructor' or 'senior instructor' in Japanese martial arts.

GS1.headline: Actor and Director Manoj Bharathiraja Passes Away at 48
GS1.explainer:
• Manoj Bharathiraja, a well-known actor and director in the Tamil film industry, passed away at the age of 48.
• Son of veteran filmmaker Bharathiraja, he made his debut as an actor and later ventured into direction.
• He was recognized for his versatile performances and his contributions to Tamil cinema in various capacities.
• His passing marks a loss for the regional film industry, with many colleagues and fans expressing condolences.
• He continued his family's legacy in filmmaking, leaving behind a body of work cherished by his audience.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
GS1.trivia: His father, Bharathiraja, is a highly acclaimed director known for his realistic portrayal of rural life.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Reinforces 'Right to Be Forgotten' in Digital Age
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court, in a landmark ruling, has clarified the scope and application of the 'Right to Be Forgotten' in India, particularly concerning public records and digital platforms.
• The judgment emphasizes balancing an individual's right to privacy with the public interest in access to information and freedom of expression.
• It mandates clear guidelines for data fiduciaries on delisting or de-indexing personal information that is no longer relevant or accurate.
• This ruling is seen as a significant step towards strengthening data protection and individual autonomy in the digital realm.
• The court highlighted the need for a comprehensive data protection law to fully address such evolving digital rights.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
Structure, organization and functioning of the Executive and the Judiciary Ministries and Departments of the Government; pressure groups and formal/informal associations and their role in the Polity.
prelimsPoints: The 'Right to Be Forgotten' is an evolving aspect of the Right to Privacy (Article 21).
It allows individuals to request removal of personal data from public view after a certain period or if it's no longer relevant.
The Supreme Court's ruling provides specific guidelines for its implementation.
It aims to balance individual privacy with freedom of speech and public information access.
mainsPoints: Analyze the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on the 'Right to Be Forgotten' for data privacy and freedom of expression in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing the 'Right to Be Forgotten' in the absence of a robust data protection framework.
Evaluate how this judgment aligns with global trends in digital rights and data governance.
GS2.imageDescription: Gavel on top of legal documents with a blurred digital background representing data.
GS2.trivia: The 'Right to Be Forgotten' originated from the European Union's General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR).

GS2.headline: India and France Deepen Strategic Ties with Focus on Indo-Pacific and Defence Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• India and France concluded their annual Strategic Dialogue, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in the Indo-Pacific region, defense, and emerging technologies.
• Discussions focused on maritime security, counter-terrorism efforts, and joint exercises, underscoring a shared vision for a free and open Indo-Pacific.
• Both nations reiterated their commitment to strengthening 'Make in India' initiatives in the defense sector, including technology transfer and co-production.
• The dialogue also covered collaboration in space, nuclear energy, and climate change, highlighting the multifaceted nature of their strategic partnership.
• This engagement reinforces India's position as a key global player and France's crucial role in India's strategic autonomy.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India and France share a long-standing strategic partnership.
The Indo-Pacific region is a key area of cooperation for both nations.
Defence collaboration includes technology transfer and joint manufacturing.
Cooperation extends to space, nuclear energy, and climate change.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in the context of evolving Indo-Pacific geopolitics.
Evaluate the role of defence cooperation in strengthening India's strategic autonomy and France's positioning in the region.
Discuss how India's bilateral relations with major powers like France contribute to its multilateral engagements and global influence.
GS2.imageDescription: Indian and French flags displayed together at a diplomatic event.
GS2.trivia: France was the first P5 nation to establish a strategic partnership with India in 1998.

GS2.headline: Government Expands 'Poshan 2.0' with Special Focus on Adolescent Health and Nutrition
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Women and Child Development has announced the expansion of the 'Poshan 2.0' mission to include a dedicated focus on improving the health and nutritional status of adolescents.
• This phase will integrate programs addressing anemia, menstrual hygiene, and nutritional awareness among adolescent girls and boys.
• The initiative aims to leverage technology for monitoring nutritional outcomes and ensuring last-mile delivery of services through Anganwadi centers and schools.
• Recognizing the critical link between adolescent health and the nation's future human capital, this move seeks to break the intergenerational cycle of malnutrition.
• Public-private partnerships and community engagement are emphasized for effective outreach and sustainable impact.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Poshan Abhiyaan (National Nutrition Mission) was launched in 2018.
Poshan 2.0 integrates various nutrition-related schemes.
This phase specifically targets adolescent health and nutrition.
Anemia and menstrual hygiene are key areas of focus for adolescent girls.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the 'Poshan 2.0' mission's expanded focus on adolescent health and nutrition in addressing India's malnutrition challenges.
Discuss the socio-economic implications of adolescent malnutrition and how targeted interventions can contribute to human capital development.
Examine the role of inter-sectoral convergence and community participation in ensuring the success of large-scale nutrition programs like Poshan Abhiyaan.
GS2.imageDescription: Group of healthy adolescents participating in an awareness program on nutrition.
GS2.trivia: India has one of the highest burdens of adolescent anemia globally.

GS2.headline: India Pushes for Coordinated G20 Action on Global Debt Crisis
GS2.explainer:
• India, as a prominent voice in the Global South, is actively advocating for a coordinated G20 approach to address the escalating global debt crisis, particularly affecting developing nations.
• During recent G20 Finance Ministers' meetings, India emphasized the need for quicker debt restructuring, increased liquidity, and sustainable financing mechanisms.
• The discussions focused on improving the Common Framework for Debt Treatments and expanding its scope to include middle-income countries.
• India also highlighted the importance of multilateral development banks playing a larger role in providing concessional financing and technical assistance.
• This push aligns with India's broader foreign policy goal of championing the interests of developing economies on global platforms.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: The G20 is an intergovernmental forum comprising 19 countries and the European Union.
India has been a strong advocate for developing countries' concerns in the G20.
The Common Framework for Debt Treatments is an initiative by the G20 and Paris Club.
Global debt crisis affects many low and middle-income countries.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's role and objectives in advocating for a coordinated G20 response to the global debt crisis, particularly for developing nations.
Discuss the challenges faced by developing countries due to unsustainable debt burdens and the limitations of existing international debt resolution mechanisms.
Evaluate the effectiveness of multilateral forums like the G20 in addressing complex global economic issues and fostering financial stability.
GS2.imageDescription: G20 logo with world map background, symbolizing global economic cooperation.
GS2.trivia: The G20 accounts for about 85% of global GDP, 75% of global trade, and two-thirds of the world's population.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Review of Implementation in Higher Education Sector
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released an interim review report on the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in the higher education sector.
• The report highlights progress in curriculum reforms, multidisciplinary education, academic bank of credits, and increased autonomy for higher education institutions.
• Challenges identified include faculty readiness, infrastructure gaps, digital divide in remote areas, and the need for greater industry-academia collaboration.
• Recommendations include accelerated faculty development programs, increased digital infrastructure investment, and enhanced funding mechanisms.
• The NEP 2020 aims to transform India's education system, making it more holistic, flexible, and aligned with 21st-century skills.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 was approved by the Union Cabinet in 2020.
It replaces the National Policy on Education of 1986.
Key features for higher education include multidisciplinary approach and Academic Bank of Credits.
The review assesses implementation progress and identifies challenges.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the progress and challenges in the implementation of National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 in the higher education sector.
Discuss how the NEP 2020 aims to address the skill gap and enhance the employability of graduates in the rapidly changing global economy.
Suggest measures to overcome the identified challenges and ensure the successful transformation of India's higher education system as envisioned by NEP 2020.
GS2.imageDescription: Students in a modern university setting, engaged in learning and discussion.
GS2.trivia: NEP 2020 aims for 50% Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) in higher education by 2035.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Achieves New Milestone in Health ID Generation
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced a significant milestone, surpassing 500 million Ayushman Bharat Health Accounts (ABHAs) or Health IDs.
• This achievement marks rapid progress in digitizing India's healthcare ecosystem, enabling seamless access to health records and services.
• ABHA acts as a unique identifier for individuals, linking their health records across different healthcare providers and allowing secure data sharing.
• The mission aims to create a comprehensive digital health infrastructure, improving efficiency, accessibility, and quality of healthcare services.
• Efforts are underway to onboard more healthcare providers and integrate various health programs under the ABDM framework.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: ABDM was launched in September 2021.
It aims to create a digital health ecosystem in India.
ABHA (Ayushman Bharat Health Account) is a unique 14-digit health ID.
It links individual health records across various healthcare providers.
mainsPoints: Assess the significance of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) in transforming India's healthcare delivery system and improving health outcomes.
Discuss the challenges involved in digitizing health records across a vast and diverse country like India, including data privacy and digital literacy.
Evaluate how the ABDM can contribute to universal health coverage and enhance the efficiency and accessibility of healthcare services for all citizens.
GS2.imageDescription: Digital interface displaying a health record with a QR code and medical symbols.
GS2.trivia: The National Health Authority (NHA) is the implementing agency for ABDM.

GS2.headline: Former Bihar MLA and Islamic Scholar Syed Ali Ashrafi Passes Away
GS2.explainer:
• Syed Ali Ashrafi, a former Member of Legislative Assembly (MLA) from Bihar and a respected Islamic scholar, passed away.
• He was known for his political career and significant contributions to religious education and community service.
• Ashrafi played a role in advocating for minority rights and promoting interfaith harmony in his region.
• His demise is mourned by political leaders and community members, recognizing his long-standing public service.
• He leaves behind a legacy of scholarship and dedication to social causes.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.
GS2.trivia: Bihar has a bicameral legislature, consisting of the Legislative Assembly (Vidhan Sabha) and the Legislative Council (Vidhan Parishad).

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Advanced Semi-Cryogenic Engine for Future Heavy-Lift Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful qualification test of its new generation semi-cryogenic engine, crucial for future heavy-lift launch vehicles.
• This engine, powered by refined kerosene (RP-1) and liquid oxygen, offers higher thrust and efficiency compared to conventional engines, reducing launch costs.
• The development is a significant step towards enhancing India's independent access to space and capability to launch heavier payloads, including future manned missions.
• It will power the future stages of India's next-generation launch vehicles, potentially replacing older configurations.
• This achievement underscores India's growing self-reliance and technological prowess in advanced rocket propulsion systems.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space.
prelimsPoints: Semi-cryogenic engines use refined kerosene (RP-1) and liquid oxygen as propellants.
They offer higher thrust and are more cost-effective than fully cryogenic engines.
ISRO's successful test aims to enhance India's heavy-lift launch capabilities.
This technology is vital for future missions, including potential human spaceflight.
mainsPoints: Assess the significance of ISRO's advancements in semi-cryogenic engine technology for India's space program and its global standing.
Discuss how indigenous development of advanced propulsion systems contributes to India's strategic autonomy in space.
Examine the potential applications and benefits of heavy-lift launch capabilities for India's economic and security interests.
GS3.imageDescription: A schematic diagram of a semi-cryogenic rocket engine being tested.
GS3.trivia: ISRO aims to integrate its semi-cryogenic engine into the future variants of the GSLV Mk III (LVM3) launch vehicle.

GS3.headline: Government Launches Framework for National Mission on Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS)
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change has unveiled a comprehensive framework for the National Mission on Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS).
• The mission aims to promote research, development, and deployment of CCUS technologies to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors like steel, cement, and power generation.
• It includes provisions for pilot projects, fiscal incentives for industrial adoption, and international collaborations for technology transfer.
• CCUS is identified as a critical tool in India's strategy to achieve its net-zero emissions targets by 2070, complementing renewable energy expansion.
• Experts highlight the need for robust regulatory mechanisms and significant investment to scale up CCUS infrastructure effectively.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: CCUS technologies aim to capture CO2 emissions from industrial sources.
Captured CO2 can be utilized for various purposes or stored underground.
The National Mission on CCUS targets hard-to-abate sectors.
It is a key component of India's strategy to achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS) technologies in achieving India's climate targets, particularly in hard-to-abate sectors.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities associated with the implementation of a national CCUS mission in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of CCUS as a climate mitigation strategy compared to renewable energy and energy efficiency measures.
GS3.imageDescription: Industrial plant with carbon capture technology chimneys releasing clean air.
GS3.trivia: Globally, CCUS projects currently capture only a small fraction of annual industrial CO2 emissions, but the technology is rapidly developing.

GS3.headline: India's Q4 FY25 GDP Growth Projections Show Robust Manufacturing Sector Performance
GS3.explainer:
• Leading economic think tanks and rating agencies have released their Q4 FY25 GDP growth projections for India, indicating continued robust expansion.
• The manufacturing sector is highlighted as a key driver, benefiting from sustained government incentives, infrastructure push, and global demand recovery.
• Services sector growth also remains strong, contributing significantly to overall economic momentum.
• While inflation remains a concern, favorable monsoon forecasts and stable global commodity prices are expected to support growth.
• These projections reinforce India's position as one of the fastest-growing major economies globally, attracting significant foreign investment.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: GDP is the total monetary or market value of all the finished goods and services produced within a country's borders in a specific time period.
The manufacturing sector's performance is a key indicator of economic health.
Government policies like PLI (Production Linked Incentive) schemes boost manufacturing.
Services sector is also a major contributor to India's GDP.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors contributing to India's sustained GDP growth in FY25, with a special focus on the manufacturing sector.
Discuss the challenges that India's economy might face despite robust growth projections, such as inflation and global economic uncertainties.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government policies in boosting manufacturing and attracting foreign direct investment.
GS3.imageDescription: Financial graphs and charts overlaid on a map of India, symbolizing economic growth.
GS3.trivia: India is projected to become the world's third-largest economy by 2027.

GS3.headline: Coastal Security Grid to be Further Strengthened with Advanced Surveillance and Interception Capabilities
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Home Affairs has announced a new phase of initiatives to bolster India's coastal security grid, drawing lessons from past threats.
• The plan involves deploying advanced surveillance technologies, including drone patrols and integrated radar systems, along the entire coastline.
• Enhanced coordination mechanisms among the Indian Navy, Coast Guard, Marine Police, and other stakeholders are a key focus.
• Training and capacity building for coastal communities and fishermen, who act as 'eyes and ears' for security forces, will be intensified.
• The move aims to prevent infiltration, smuggling, and maritime terrorism, safeguarding India's vast coastline and economic interests.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate.
prelimsPoints: India has a coastline of approximately 7,516 km.
Coastal security involves multiple agencies like the Indian Navy, Coast Guard, and Marine Police.
Advanced surveillance technologies are being integrated into the coastal security framework.
Community participation, especially from fishermen, is crucial for effective coastal security.
mainsPoints: Discuss the multifaceted challenges to India's coastal security and the measures being taken to address them.
Evaluate the role of technology and inter-agency coordination in strengthening India's maritime domain awareness and response capabilities.
Examine how community involvement can be effectively leveraged to enhance coastal security and counter illicit activities.
GS3.imageDescription: Indian Coast Guard patrol boat on surveillance duty in coastal waters.
GS3.trivia: India's coastline spans nine states and four Union Territories.

GS3.headline: Horticulture Sector Mourns Demise of Dr. Krishna Lal Chadha, 'Father of Indian Horticulture'
GS3.explainer:
• Dr. Krishna Lal Chadha, renowned horticultural scientist and former Deputy Director General (Horticulture) of ICAR, passed away at 88.
• He was widely regarded as the 'Father of Indian Horticulture' for his pioneering contributions to research, development, and policy-making in the field.
• His work significantly boosted the production and productivity of fruits, vegetables, and ornamental crops across India.
• Dr. Chadha played a pivotal role in establishing several horticultural research institutions and promoting commercial horticulture.
• His legacy continues to influence agricultural practices and food security initiatives in the country.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System— objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: Dr. Krishna Lal Chadha was a renowned horticultural scientist.
He served as Deputy Director General (Horticulture) of ICAR.
He is known for his significant contributions to Indian horticulture.
His work focused on increasing fruit, vegetable, and ornamental crop production.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of scientific research and agricultural scientists like Dr. K.L. Chadha in transforming India's horticulture sector.
Analyze the importance of horticulture in ensuring food security, farmer income, and agricultural diversification in India.
Examine the challenges and opportunities in promoting sustainable and commercial horticulture practices in the country.
GS3.imageDescription: Portrait of Dr. Krishna Lal Chadha with a background of vibrant fruit orchards.
GS3.trivia: Dr. Chadha was instrumental in the development of new varieties of mango, grapes, and litchi.

GS3.headline: Government Considers New Policy Framework to Regulate Generative AI
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) is reportedly drafting a comprehensive policy framework to regulate Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The framework aims to address concerns related to ethical AI development, data privacy, misinformation, copyright infringement, and job displacement.
• It is expected to propose guidelines for AI developers, deployers, and users, fostering responsible innovation while mitigating potential risks.
• Consultations with industry experts, academia, and civil society organizations are underway to ensure a balanced and future-ready policy.
• India seeks to position itself as a global leader in ethical AI, leveraging its digital public infrastructure while ensuring user safety and trust.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Generative AI refers to AI models that can generate new content like text, images, or code.
MeitY is the nodal ministry for IT and electronics policy in India.
The new policy aims to balance innovation with ethical concerns and risks.
Issues like data privacy, misinformation, and intellectual property are key considerations.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical and regulatory challenges posed by the rapid advancement of Generative AI and the need for a comprehensive policy framework in India.
Analyze how India can leverage its digital public infrastructure to foster responsible AI innovation while mitigating risks like misinformation and job displacement.
Evaluate the potential impact of AI regulation on India's technology sector and its ambitions to become a global AI hub.
GS3.imageDescription: Abstract representation of AI neural networks and data flowing through digital circuits.
GS3.trivia: Generative AI models like ChatGPT and Midjourney gained widespread public attention in 2022-2023.

GS3.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Shows Progress in Pilot Projects and Policy Incentives
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy released a progress report on the National Green Hydrogen Mission, highlighting significant strides in pilot project implementation.
• Several large-scale green hydrogen production facilities are under construction, leveraging India's renewable energy potential.
• The mission's policy incentives, including SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme, are attracting private investment and fostering technological innovation.
• Focus remains on reducing production costs, developing robust storage and transportation infrastructure, and promoting indigenous manufacturing of electrolyzers.
• Green hydrogen is envisioned as a crucial element for decarbonizing hard-to-abate industrial sectors and achieving energy independence.
gsSubTopics: Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced by electrolysis of water using renewable energy.
The National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
The SIGHT programme provides financial incentives for green hydrogen manufacturing.
It is crucial for decarbonizing industries like refining, fertilizer, and steel.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the progress and potential of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving energy security and climate goals.
Discuss the technological and economic challenges in scaling up green hydrogen production, storage, and distribution infrastructure in India.
Analyze the role of government incentives and private sector investment in driving the green hydrogen ecosystem and promoting indigenous manufacturing.
GS3.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen production facility with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
